states.whyNow: Iowa is a clean Midwest gap where county records and permit-file quality can create a better wedge than pretending there is one statewide install story.
states.launchAngle: Target angle: county records path and file quality before the buyer or owner trusts a flat estimate.
researchTasks: Find the statewide homeowner-facing onsite wastewater or county-records entry point.
Collect county or local office routing that determines who actually holds the file.
Pull official language on permit copies, inspections, or records lookup that a homeowner can safely use.

states.whyNow: Kansas can likely support a soil and site-risk wedge that is stronger than another generic install-cost state page.
states.launchAngle: Target angle: soil, perc, and system-path risk before the homeowner assumes a standard install route.
researchTasks: Find the statewide homeowner entry point for onsite wastewater permits or site evaluation.
Collect public source language on soil, site, lagoon, or alternative-system risk that homeowners can use.
Map any county or local-office handoff that changes the first call.

states.whyNow: Nebraska is a broad plains gap where permit-path and county routing likely matter more than a fake statewide tank table.
states.launchAngle: Target angle: permit path, county routing, and file quality before install quotes harden.
researchTasks: Find the statewide homeowner-facing onsite wastewater or permit guidance hub.
Collect county or local office routing that determines the real permit office.
Pull a homeowner-safe permit sequence that can anchor a permit page without overclaiming.

states.whyNow: New Mexico can likely support a buyer-risk and records wedge where local file quality matters more than broad averages.
states.launchAngle: Target angle: buyer diligence, local file quality, and records retrieval before the seller story sets the anchor.
researchTasks: Find the statewide homeowner-facing entry point for septic compliance, permits, or property-transfer context.
Collect county or local office routing that determines who controls the file.
Pull official language on transfer or inspection triggers that is safe to publish for buyer pages.

states.whyNow: Utah can likely support a permit-first wedge where local health routing matters more than a generic install estimate.
states.launchAngle: Target angle: permit path and local-office handoff before the homeowner trusts a flat install range.
researchTasks: Find the statewide homeowner entry point for septic permitting and approvals.

states.whyNow: West Virginia keeps the eastern buildout moving and likely supports a records-first wedge where county file quality matters before the homeowner trusts the low end.
states.launchAngle: Target angle: permit-file retrieval and local office confirmation before the homeowner treats the quote as settled.
researchTasks: Find the statewide homeowner guidance for onsite sewage permits, inspections, or local health routing.
Collect any permit-copy, file-request, or records lookup workflow that a homeowner can actually use.
Document the safest local-authority explanation that can later support both records and permit pages.
